name: Scrapy
summary: Fast, batteries-included web scraping and crawling framework for Python.

story.problem: requests + BeautifulSoup works for one page, but you need to scrape 10,000 product pages, follow pagination, handle retries, avoid getting banned, store results to a database, and run it on a schedule. Doing all that by hand is a full project in itself.
story.mental_model: Scrapy is a framework, not a library. You define Spiders (what to crawl and how to parse), Items (what data to extract), and Pipelines (what to do with it). Scrapy's async engine handles concurrency, retries, politeness delays, and deduplication — you just write the parsing logic.
story.when_to_use: Large-scale crawling, production spiders that run regularly, structured data extraction with pipelines (to DB, CSV, JSON), sites with pagination or login.
story.when_not_to_use: Single-page quick scrapes (use requests+BS4). JavaScript-heavy sites (use Playwright; or scrapy-playwright to integrate). When a proper API exists.

alternatives.name: requests + BS4
alternatives.reason: Simpler for one-off scrapes, but no concurrency, retries, or pipelines built in.

alternatives.name: playwright
alternatives.reason: Required for JS-rendered pages; can be integrated with Scrapy via scrapy-playwright.

alternatives.name: apify
alternatives.reason: Managed scraping platform — handles infrastructure, proxies, and scheduling for you.

nodes.label: scrapy
nodes.summary: Framework package. Import Spider, Item, Field, Request, etc.

nodes.label: scrapy.Spider
nodes.summary: Base class for all spiders. Define name, start_urls, and parse() to extract data and yield more Requests.

nodes.label: CrawlSpider + Rules
nodes.summary: Auto-follows links matching Rules/LinkExtractors. Good for crawling whole sites.

nodes.label: scrapy.Request
nodes.summary: Represents a URL to fetch. Yield from parse() to queue more pages. Supports callback, meta, headers.

nodes.label: Response
nodes.summary: Scrapy response object. .css(), .xpath() for selecting. .follow() for relative links. .text, .url.

nodes.label: scrapy.Item
nodes.summary: Dict-like container for scraped data. Defines allowed fields. Passed through Pipelines.

nodes.label: Item Pipelines
nodes.summary: Process Items after extraction: validate, deduplicate, save to DB or file. Defined in pipelines.py.

nodes.label: Middlewares
nodes.summary: Downloader middlewares: user-agent rotation, proxy handling, retry logic, cookies.

nodes.label: Settings
nodes.summary: settings.py controls: DOWNLOAD_DELAY, CONCURRENT_REQUESTS, ROBOTSTXT_OBEY, pipelines, middlewares.

steps.label: Create project
steps.code:
scrapy startproject myproject
cd myproject
scrapy genspider quotes quotes.toscrape.com

steps.label: Write spider

tasks.title: Save items to a database via Pipeline

steps.label: Pipeline
